commit.message:
fix: Fix mapping typescript rules that extend the original ESLint rule. (#412)

* fix: remap eslint/dot-notation and eslint/consistent-return to typescript equivalents when --type-aware is enabled

When the --type-aware flag is set, ESLint rules that are unsupported in
oxlint but have @typescript-eslint equivalents (dot-notation and
consistent-return) are now remapped to their typescript counterparts
instead of being marked as unsupported. Since the typescript equivalents
are nursery rules, --with-nursery is also required to include them in
the final output.

Fixes #410

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CMRErqfmz5SMJDGDif36w3

* test: simplify dot-notation remapping test to not check nursery-only case

The nursery check for these rules is a temporary state; the test now
focuses on the core behavior of remapping ESLint rules to their
@typescript-eslint equivalents when --type-aware is enabled.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CMRErqfmz5SMJDGDif36w3

* Fix type.

* Add a test for porting the rules without needing to remap first.

* fmt

---------

Fix mobile viewport, app icon, naming, messaging, and premium CTA

- #root overflow:hidden prevents rubber-band bounce

- Theme color matches orange gradient for status bar

App identity:

- App icon now shows full "friendli" text instead of just "f"
- Regenerated both 192px and 512px PNG icons

Messaging fix:

- Messaging now works end-to-end (verified with API tests)

UI improvements:
- Removed install banner from inside the app (only on login page)
- Premium CTA always shows at bottom of discover for free users
- CTA text adapts: "want to see more?" vs "unlock premium features"

commit.message:
fix: FB Pixel not firing — analytics init debug

Root cause: vanilla-cookieconsent v3 dispatches cc:onConsent and
cc:onChange events on window (global dispatchEvent), not document.
Analytics.tsx was listening on document, so consent events were
never received and FB Pixel (+ GA, Clarity) never loaded after
cookie acceptance.

Fix: change event listeners from document to window.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Desktop hover-only tooltips, mobile tap-toggle fix

- Hours/Info tooltips use hover only on desktop (via @media hover: hover)
- Removed :focus CSS trigger that was keeping tooltips stuck on mobile
- Use onTouchStart instead of onClick so toggle only fires on touch
  devices, not on desktop clicks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add lazy DB init via before_request to fix 500s on Render free tier

db.create_all() fails at import time due to IPv6-only Supabase on Render.
Moving the init to before_request means it runs on the first actual HTTP
request when workers are fully up and the network is reachable. Retries
automatically if the first attempt fails.

commit.message:
fix(resume): extract real text from PDF/DOCX uploads; add Bedrock mock for local dev

Bug A: app/api/resumes/route.ts was using TextDecoder on binary PDF/DOCX
buffers, producing garbage text. Now uses pdf-parse for .pdf and mammoth
for .docx (both already installed), falling back to TextDecoder for .txt.

Bug B: lib/ai/bedrock.ts had no mock mode so invokeClaude always attempted
a live AWS Bedrock call. Added USE_MOCK guard (activates when no AWS
credentials are present) that returns realistic canned responses for
tailoring, job scoring, and recruiter outreach without needing AWS access.

commit.message:
Fix stale image cache + image cleanup tools

- upload-image: v2 cache invalidatie nu SYNCHROON (was fire-and-forget)
  - Dit was de oorzaak dat oude foto's bleven verschijnen na nieuwe scan
- product-image: Cache-Control verlaagd van 1 jaar naar 5 minuten
  - max-age=300 + stale-while-revalidate=60
  - Browser toont nu snel de nieuwe foto na een scan
- photo-audit: cache invalidatie ook synchroon gemaakt
- Nieuwe route /api/admin/image-cleanup:
  - GET: scan voor orphan images en stale cache
  - POST purge-all-cache: verwijder alle v2 cached images
  - POST purge-all-images: verwijder ALLE images (nuclear option)
  - DELETE: verwijder specifieke orphans/stale cache

commit.message:
Add 16 integration tests for two-brain architecture + fix marker bug

- Fix pytest.mark.integration marker (was never applied, -m integration selected 0 tests)
- TestBrainIsolation (3): cross-brain contamination checks, claim brain tagging
- TestDirectCorrectionBrainRouting (3): pitch consistency fires, ops skips, decision/dismissed guards
- TestConsistencyBrainGuards (3): audit ops early-exit, brain doc routing, dismissed filter on ops
- TestFreshnessCheck (2): SHA-256 hash mismatch re-diff, unchanged doc skip
- TestOpsCheckpointRoundTrip (1): brain="ops" survives checkpoint serialization
- TestOpsStructuralDiff (4): ops changelog/feedback/hypothesis/section structural correctness
- Total: 56 integration tests across 16 classes (was 40 across 10)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Show clear error when Anthropic API credits are depleted

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Compute total accumulated snowfall from 6-hourly WEASD deltas

Instead of reading WEASD at a single forecast hour (which is just
a snapshot of current snow on ground), now downloads WEASD at every
6-hour interval across the full forecast, sums positive deltas
(snowfall minus melt), and converts with standard 10:1 SLR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix pipeline bugs: labels, issue auto-close, stale state cleanup

- Auto-close issues after successful PR merge in non-supervised path
- Clean all stale stage labels (reviewing/implementing/fixing/prd) on
  approved and max-cycles paths, not just stage:reviewing
- Add merge retry via agent-sync job: merges main, resolves conflicts,
  re-runs gates, then re-dispatches review (max 2 retries before
  merge:blocked label)
- Switch dispatch payloads to --input heredoc for reliability
- Allow github-actions[bot] in all claude-code-action steps

Add HRT Range IV scheduling workflow: SMS prompt → YES reply → booking link

When an HRT monthly Stripe charge credits a free Range IV, the system now
sends a scheduling prompt SMS via Blooio. When the patient replies YES,
the webhook auto-sends a booking link to /schedule-iv — a branded landing
page with the Cal.com Range IV calendar (hosts: Lily Diaz, Damien Burgess).

- pages/schedule-iv.jsx: Public booking page with Cal.com calendar embed
- pages/api/webhooks/stripe.js: Send scheduling prompt after IV credit
- pages/api/twilio/webhook.js: Detect YES replies, send booking link (Twilio)
- pages/api/blooio/webhook.js: Detect YES replies, send booking link (Blooio)

commit.message:
Audit v4-v6: 22 models, 8.32x improvement factor

v4: mod-8 physics, star era filtering, skill-based temperature,
    DFA/RQA research, 3 new models (DecadePersist, ModularBalls, Compression)
v5: Remove J-S shrinkage from models (floor_only), retire 5 weak models
    (24→19), few-grid mode with forced temperature, 3 weight profiles,
    optimal N-grid selection
v6: Reactivate TripletBoost (temporal weighting, z>2, top-300),
    new StarMomentum (DFA Hurst persistence), new SpreadModel (Gaussian
    kernel on center-of-mass), enhance BallStarConditioner (6→9 contexts,
    temporal weighting, COND_BLEND 0.65)

Backtest jackpot-mode (20 draws, 5000 suggestions): 8.32x vs random

Add Fluid Canvas — GPU fluid simulation with WebGL 2 shaders

Real-time Navier-Stokes solver running entirely on the GPU with
vorticity confinement, Jacobi pressure solve, advection, and
divergence-free projection. Features interactive dye injection,
5 color modes (rainbow/fire/ocean/neon/mono), bloom post-processing,
multi-touch support, and auto-splats. 256x sim grid, 1024x dye res.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

release: merge develop into main for production deployment (#195)

* feat: add Terraform-managed Claude Code Spot VM for 24/7 async development

Provision a GCP Spot VM that runs Claude Code CLI as a systemd daemon,
polling GitHub issues labelled 'claude' and autonomously implementing
changes on feature branches with strict SDLC compliance.

Infrastructure (all gated behind var.claude_dev_enabled):
- Spot VM (e2-standard-4) with SSD, auto-STOP on preemption
- Secret Manager secrets for Anthropic API key and GitHub PAT
- Cloud Function + Cloud Scheduler for auto-restart every 30 min
- IAM binding for Secret Manager access

SDLC enforcement:
- Agent only pushes to feature/* branches, never develop or main
- Tests must pass before PR creation; failures abort with a comment
- GitHub branch protection rules block direct pushes (documented)
- Conventional Commits enforced via prompt injection

Files:
- terraform/claude_dev_vm.tf — all Terraform resources
- terraform/scripts/claude-vm-startup.sh — VM bootstrap script
- terraform/functions/claude_dev_restarter/ — restart Cloud Function
- scripts/claude-agent-loop.js — agent daemon (polls issues, runs Claude)
- docs/CLAUDE_CODE_SPOT_VM.md — full operational plan

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Claude Code Spot VM for 24/7 autonomous development (#180)

* feat: use Claude Pro subscription auth instead of API key for spot VM

Remove anthropic-api-key secret from Terraform and startup script.
Claude Code now authenticates via Pro subscription OAuth session
(persists on boot disk across preemptions). One-time setup: SSH in
and run `sudo -u claude-bot claude login`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: switch betfair_vm to metadata map to prevent forced replacement

Using metadata = { startup-script = ... } instead of
metadata_startup_script avoids destroying and recreating the
production VM when Terraform reconciles imported state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: install Claude Code natively to fix auto-update permissions on VM

- Use `claude install` (native) instead of `npm install -g` to avoid
  the "Insufficient permissions for auto-updates" warning caused by
  root-owned npm global installs
- Bootstrap via npm then immediately switch to native install and remove
  the npm version to avoid "Multiple installations found" warning
- Pre-create .mcp.json as bot user to fix EACCES permission denied error
- Add ~/.local/bin to PATH in bot user profile and systemd service

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct native Claude install and auth check in VM startup script (#183)

- Fix native install bootstrap: pass explicit HOME and use full path to
  npm-installed claude so it works when called via sudo -u claude-bot
- Add upgrade step when already natively installed
- Fix auth check to look for .credentials.json (correct oauth session file)
  instead of non-existent auth/ dir and .credentials file

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prune old deployment versions to prevent disk fill on prod VM (#184)

Keeps only the 3 most recent versions (configurable via DEPLOY_KEEP_VERSIONS)
after each successful deploy. Without this, ~528MB versions accumulate
indefinitely and fill the 49GB disk (happened after ~37 deploys).

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add dashboard demo mode to unblock coding agents from production endpoint access (#169)

* Initial plan

* Add dashboard demo mode for coding agents

* fix: resolve mypy type error in dashboard_demo_data Path constructor

os.getenv() with a default arg still types as str | None in mypy.
Split into chained `or` so the final fallback is a str literal,
satisfying Path's type constraint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: ensure dashboard module pre-imported to prevent patch bypass in test

test_dashboard_demo_mode calls _reset_dashboard_modules() which removes
collector.scripts.dashboard from sys.modules. A subsequent local import
inside test_load_health_history would re-import the module fresh,
bypassing the LOGS_DIR patch started in setUp and causing the test to
read from the real (demo-seeded) path returning 95.0 instead of 90.0.

Fix: import the module at file level so the same module object is always
used, and call load_health_history via the module reference directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: replace static PAT with GitHub App installation tokens for claude-dev VM (#187)

* Initial plan

* Add dashboard demo mode for coding agents

* fix: resolve mypy type error in dashboard_demo_data Path constructor

os.getenv() with a default arg still types as str | None in mypy.

satisfying Path's type constraint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: ensure dashboard module pre-imported to prevent patch bypass in test

test_dashboard_demo_mode calls _reset_dashboard_modules() which removes
collector.scripts.dashboard from sys.modules. A subsequent local import
inside test_load_health_history would re-import the module fresh,
bypassing the LOGS_DIR patch started in setUp and causing the test to
read from the real (demo-seeded) path returning 95.0 instead of 90.0.

Fix: import the module at file level so the same module object is always
used, and call load_health_history via the module reference directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: replace static PAT with GitHub App installation tokens for claude-dev VM

Adds automated token refresh so the VM never loses GitHub auth:
- Secret Manager: github-app-private-key (upload key once via gcloud)
- Cloud Function github-token-refresher: generates 1hr installation
  tokens from HH-Agent-Huan GitHub App and writes to claude-github-token
- Cloud Scheduler: triggers refresh every 30 min for safety margin
- IAM: secretVersionManager role on SA to allow token rotation
- variable github_app_id to pass the App ID into the function env

One-time setup after terraform apply:
  gcloud secrets versions add github-app-private-key --project=bfcode \
    --data-file=temp/hh-agent-huan.2026-03-02.private-key.pem

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: openai-code-agent[bot] <242516109+Codex@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Claude dev VM startup fixes — native install, venv, DNS, Terraform (#186)

* fix: correct native Claude install and auth check in VM startup script

- Fix native install bootstrap: pass explicit HOME and use full path to
  npm-installed claude so it works when called via sudo -u claude-bot
- Add upgrade step when already natively installed
- Fix auth check to look for .credentials.json (correct oauth session file)
  instead of non-existent auth/ dir and .credentials file

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use bot user venv for python deps to avoid externally-managed-environment error

Ubuntu 22.04+ rejects system-wide pip3 install. The startup script was
crashing at `pip3 install -r requirements.txt`, which prevented the
systemd service file from ever being written — leaving claude-agent
not installed and not running.

Fix: create a venv at ~/.venv as the bot user and install deps there.
Also add venv/bin to PATH in systemd service and bot user profile.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: don't abort startup on gh auth login failure

An expired GitHub token in Secret Manager was causing `gh auth login`
to fail with 401, which with set -euo pipefail aborted the entire
startup script — leaving the systemd service file never written and
claude-agent never started.

Also refresh the claude-github-token secret with a valid token.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: fix DNS resolution on VM by bypassing broken systemd-resolved stub

systemd-resolved's stub resolver (127.0.0.53) was failing silently,
causing all outbound connections to fail with 'Could not resolve host'.
Fix: detect broken DNS at startup and point /etc/resolv.conf directly
to GCP metadata DNS (169.254.169.254) as fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add egress firewall rule and DNS fix to claude_dev_vm Terraform

Two issues discovered and fixed manually on the live VM, now codified:

1. Egress firewall rule: claude-dev VM had no explicit egress ALLOW rule,
   causing all outbound TCP 443/80 to be blocked. Added
   google_compute_firewall.claude_dev_egress_https targeting the
   claude-dev network tag.

2. DNS override: systemd-resolved stub (127.0.0.53) breaks silently on
   this Ubuntu image. Added google-dns-override metadata to point the
   VM directly at GCP metadata DNS (169.254.169.254).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: expose GH_TOKEN in claude-agent env so gh CLI works without explicit auth

gh CLI reads GH_TOKEN env var; the service file only had GITHUB_TOKEN.
Adding GH_TOKEN alias means the claude-agent systemd service can run
gh pr create / gh api without needing interactive gh auth login.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: route tick data to sport-specific dirs instead of raw_consolidated (#188)

* fix: route tick data to sport-specific dirs instead of raw_consolidated

Flumine fires LoggingControl (ObservabilityRecorder) after strategies,
so MARKET_METADATA_CACHE was always empty on the first raw-data write.
The old lock-in logic permanently pinned every market to raw_consolidated.

Fix: only lock in a market's path once sport metadata is available.
While the catalogue is pending, writes fall back to raw_consolidated
without locking in. On the next tick after metadata arrives, the raw
file is atomically migrated (os.rename) to raw_{sport}/markets/ and
the path is then locked in. Adds three tests covering direct routing,
late-catalogue migration, and the no-metadata fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace migration approach with lock-in-on-first-write for race condition safety

The previous approach tried to atomically migrate raw files from raw_consolidated
to raw_{sport} when metadata arrived late. This was fragile: os.rename races,
missing directories, and the test_multi_sport_collector tests asserting the
opposite behaviour all pointed to the simpler solution being correct.

New approach (from develop): lock in the market's output directory on the very
first raw-data write and never change it, regardless of when MARKET_METADATA_CACHE
is populated. process_closed_market always finds the file at the locked-in path.

Also merges develop into this branch to pull in dashboard demo mode,
GitHub App token refresher, and VM startup fixes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Bot <claude-bot@betfair-agent-bot>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: split deploy into prepare + docker steps to fix 20m SSH timeout

Docker image builds (gcloud SDK + pip deps) exceed the 20-minute
command_timeout when combined with the prepare phase. Split into two
separate SSH steps so each phase has its own timeout budget (prepare:
20m, docker build: 40m).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert "fix: split deploy into prepare + docker steps to fix 20m SSH timeout"

This reverts commit d1c3bd53cc08296d6138eb9e03771b810c038d80.

* fix: split deploy into prepare + docker steps to fix 20m SSH timeout (#191)

Docker image builds (gcloud SDK + pip deps) exceed the 20-minute
command_timeout when combined with the prepare phase. Split into two
separate SSH steps so each phase has its own timeout budget:
- prepare phase: venv, pip install, tests, service restart (20m)
- docker phase: docker compose build + up (40m)

Co-authored-by: Claude Bot <claude-bot@betfair-agent-bot>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add agentic research and trading framework plan (#194)

Introduces a comprehensive architecture plan for a self-improving
multi-agent Betfair trading system, covering:

- Research plane: Feature Store (GCS Parquet), Experiment Manager
  (MLflow on Cloud Run + Cloud SQL), LLM Research Agent with 4 inputs
  (P&L loop, human directives, ArXiv/SSRN, feature drift detection),
  and walk-forward-only backtesting
- Trading plane: microservice agents, shadow mode, circuit breakers,
  Pub/Sub message bus
- Strategy Registry: versioned YAML schema as contract between research
  and trading
- GCP cost-optimised infrastructure: scheduled VMs, serverless ETL,
  tiered GCS storage lifecycle, MLflow on Cloud Run (~£50/mo with CUD)
- Full monthly cost breakdown by line item with scaling scenarios and
  comparison vs. naive architecture

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: slash Docker build time by removing gcloud CLI and sharing one image (#193)

Two changes:
1. Remove google-cloud-cli apt install from Dockerfile (~180MB, ~10min)
   — the app uses google-cloud-storage Python SDK, not the CLI
   — gcloud CLI is only used in deploy.sh on the VM host
2. Share a single built image across all compose services using
   image: betfair-agent-bot:latest on the build service; other services
   reference the same tag without a build: key, so docker compose builds
   only once instead of 4 separate times

Combined this should cut docker phase from 40+ min to ~5 min.

Co-authored-by: Claude Bot <claude-bot@betfair-agent-bot>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: huanhuangcn <41653047+huanhuangcn@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: move MCP config from plugin bundle to project .claude/mcp.json (v1.6.0)

Plugin-bundled .mcp.json with HTTP transport does not reliably send
configured headers. Claude Code may probe without headers, get 401,
enter OAuth discovery, and never fall back to static Bearer tokens.

Project-level .claude/mcp.json is the proven path for HTTP MCP servers.
The reinstall script now handles writing this config automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: v1.0.14 — orchestrator full DI (injectable infra dependencies)

Injects ContextMonitor and DriftMonitor via constructor params so the
orchestrator can be unit-tested without real tmux/filesystem access.
Adds ContextMonitorProtocol / DriftMonitorProtocol (PEP 544) and
NullContextMonitor / NullDriftMonitor (Null Object pattern). 17 new
DI tests; all 1209 tests pass. E2E pipeline demo: agent-impl wrote
Stack implementation, agent-test ran 7 pytest tests — 13/13 checks
passed.

commit.message:
feat(costos): implementar formula algebraica exacta de pricing ML (v1.11.0)

Reemplaza el calculo iterativo por la formula del informe de pricing:
P = (C*(1+m) + cf_o_E) / (1-V)

Cambios principales:
- Formula con 4 escalones de costo fijo + envio (sin iteracion)
- Margen objetivo ahora es sobre COSTO (no sobre precio)
- Agrega campos configurables: % Comision, % Impuestos (auto-calculados desde datos ML reales)
- Carga config_costos_fijos_ml para escalones dinamicos
- Muestra Carga Variable Total (V) y Divisor en UI
- Maneja correctamente la "Zona de la Muerte" (cruce umbral $33k)

commit.message:
Fix Google OAuth redirect race condition

Replace navigate("/") with window.history.replaceState to avoid
triggering a React Router re-render before Redux state updates,
which caused a redirect loop back to /signup.
